title: meum athamanticum subsp. nevadense
intro: Meum athamanticum subsp. nevadense, also known as Nevada melissa or Nevadan meadow balm, is a subspecies of the meadow balm plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Meum athamanticum subsp. nevadense include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and support respiratory health. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it can also be used topically as a salve or ointment to apply directly to the affected area.
